highlights.text: Given the powerful bullish trigger here and seasonal strength, I was looking for higher, writing last Tuesday December 23rd at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: Begins under 6935-38.” We spent today grinding lower slowly, back-testing 6935 from below this afternoon then resuming to sub 6900.

Whenever there is what I call an elevator down sell (a vertical flush that cuts through every support - this is the first subling) bulls always get nearly every point of that type of action paid back in the form of a squeeze ( the squeeze being the second sibling ).
That footprint is the Failed Breakdown.
This would mean bulls will get paid back for this action, but only when a Failed Breakdown occurs.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a powerful Failed Breakdown we saw last Tuesday (given to readers in advance) that produced the 70 point Christmas Eve/Christmas Day rip into last weeks highs.

post_title: 2025 Concludes For SPX with 4 Red Day Dip. Will Buyers Return in January? Jan 2nd Plan
